caption:
Stop making API routes for simple form submissions.

React 19 Server Actions handle it in 3 lines.
No fetch. No API route. No loading state boilerplate.

📌 SAVE this — your Next.js code is about to get 80% cleaner.

Are you using Server Actions yet? Comment 👇

#react #nextjs #javascript #typescript #webdev #coding #programming #react19 #serveractions #frontend #codingtips

slides.title: Which snippet is better?
slides.problem: In Next.js, submit a form that saves data to the database.

a.label: API Route + fetch

b.label: Server Action

slides.title: B. Not even close.

verdicts.title: 11 lines of boilerplate
verdicts.text: You need useState for loading, event.preventDefault, JSON.stringify, a separate /api/user route file, error handling, and response parsing. Every form needs this dance.

verdicts.title: 3 lines. No API route needed.
verdicts.text: The function runs on the server automatically. Next.js handles the network request, loading states (via useFormStatus), error boundaries, and revalidation. Zero boilerplate.

slides.takeaway: Server Actions killed API routes for mutations.

slides.title: The full pattern
slides.sub: A complete form with Server Action + loading state:
slides.note: No fetch. No useState. No API route. The form just works.

slides.title: Server Actions cheat sheet

items.text: 'use server' at top of function → runs on server
revalidatePath() → refreshes cached data after mutation
No /api routes needed for form submissions anymore

slides.question: Are you still writing API routes for forms? 👇
